# 12 H. Cao et al.

# 5.1 Load-Aware Matching Algorithm

Step 3. If an RSU has the highest load and not all unmatched tasks prefer it, skip it and continue matching with other RSUs (line 10 to line 13).

Step 4. The current RSU selects its most preferred task from proposals and checks if it meets the execution requirements. If met, schedule the task on it. Otherwise, reject the task and all less preferred tasks (line 14 to line 26).

Step 5. Repeat steps 2 to 4, until all tasks are matched.

Algorithm 1: LAMA: Load-Aware Matching Algorithm
Require: Set I of all inference requests and set R of RSUs
Ensure: A stable matching μ between R and I to get models deployment and task offloading strategy
1: Initialize I′ := I, Lcur := 0;
2: Sort tasks by increasing maximum tolerated delay;
3:  while I′ = ∅ do
4:   for r ∈ R and ajk ∈ I′ do
5:    Build preference lists: Pₐ jk and Pᵣ ;
6:   end for
7:   for ajk ∈ I′ do
8:    Task ajk proposes to its most preferred RSU r∗;
9:   end for
10:   for r ∈ R do
11:    if  Lᵣ = Lᵐᵃˣ    = 0 and ∃ajk ∈ I′, r∗ ≻ₐ r then
12:        Skip r;cur                         jk
13:    end if
14:    Select most preferred task ajk from proposals;
15:    if  r can execute ajk then
16:        if  no suitable model deployed then
17:            Deploy the model with accuracy lᵐⁱⁿ;
18:        end if
19:        Schedule ajk to r and remove ajk from I′;
20:        Update Lᵣ and Lcur;
21:    else
22:        Reject ajk    and all less preferred tasks in proposals;
23:        Rejected tasks remove r from their preference lists;
24:    end if
25:    end for
26:  end while

# 5.2 GPU-Accelerated Task Processing Algorithm

Our proposed GAP algorithm establishes a task-to-device selection strategy, determining which tasks should be scheduled to GPUs for execution, while further ensuring load balancing across the entire system. The detailed procedure of GAP is described in algorithm 2. Specifically, GAP works as follows.

Step 1. Identify the dominant task type and its maximum accuracy requirement for the current RSU equipped with a GPU (line 2).
